desc: The rail for the byte door lens gate passes with the production fix reverted, proven by running it. The fixture appends only a primitive observation and never appends any bytes, so every content address the tests name is unfindable and the door returns the same not found answer down the missing reference path no matter what the gate decides. The whole file also survives replacing the door body with an unconditional refusal.

The fixture's redacted sibling reading is not actually redacted. It omits one property from its props map but declares the same default policy, and the resolution engine falls back to the default for any property the schema does not name. Both readings therefore resolve to identical views, so the fixture cannot express the disclosure it claims to test even in principle.

The second production site changed by this ticket has no rail at all. Every existing fixture that reaches this path builds worlds where the program name and the lens name are equal by construction, so the changed comparison computes the same boolean either way and the fix could be reverted silently with the suite still green.

Public version pins are resolved by filtering registrations on the program name and then indexing by position. Since coexistence landed, that list interleaves every reading over the program, so the nth entry is not the nth version of the lens the operator named. An operator who narrows a reading and declares its new version public can silently freeze a pin onto the wider earlier version, which the anonymous door then serves. This is the site that mints the authorization string the read side checks.

The route door authorizes on the pair of lens name and version address, then resolves the version by content address alone without asserting the resolved version belongs to the lens it authorized. The equivalent rest path already checks both halves, so this path is the outlier. Chained with the pin miscount on the declaration side, the two compose into anonymous service of an undeclared reading without anyone acting maliciously.

The last remaining comparison against a program name in the gateway sits among siblings that all compare lens names. It is not an authorization decision today because materializations are per program by design, but the recorded tell for this hazard class is exactly a program name comparison sitting among lens name siblings, so leaving it undocumented invites a future reader to copy it into a path where the distinction decides something.

The patch script replaces only the first occurrence of its target pattern and then treats the presence of any patched text as proof the whole job is done, so a second unpatched call site would be left broken while every later run reports already applied and exits clean. There is also no verification after the write despite the header claiming the script verifies before and after. This is the completeness hazard the repo already recorded, appearing in the script whose purpose is unblocking the independent reviewer.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard one, originally found before the ledger existed. Suppression ranges over the operand set rather than being carried by the delta, so any operation that narrows a delta set and keeps a claim while dropping the negation that struck it revives that claim as live. Nothing errors and the result is well formed and wrong. It cost three sites including a federation facing one where peers received claims without their retractions.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard two. A trusted set resolved live inside a lens reaches exactly one link because the view predicate is stratified at depth one permanently by substrate design. Code that writes a recursive authority chain expecting the lens to walk it gets a silently shallow answer. The remedy is to flatten on the binding path or derive an author and read its emissions, never to cache the flattened result.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard three. A gather body's child reading lives in the body rather than in the lens, and bodies are content addressed and portable, so sibling lenses over one hyperschema necessarily share the child reading and two parties adopting the same published body share its readings even in different stores. The belief that separate stores mean separate readings is false in a way that reads as obviously true.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard four. A delta content address commits to author and timestamp as well as pointers, so two parties asserting the same fact mint different deltas. Any scheme assuming the same claim yields the same identifier across authors is broken, including existence by construction checks, cross party deduplication, and asking whether someone already said this. It is also why promotion must inherit the source timestamp to stay idempotent. Path corrected on record: the promotion code lives in adopt.ts.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard five, forward looking rather than yet realized. From substrate version zero point nine, parsing fails closed on unknown keys and not merely unknown tags, so a stale or misspelled key that used to be dropped silently will throw by name. This host generates terms in at least four places, and a term only built on an uncommon path will not be exercised by the suite, so the upgrade needs a deliberate pass over every generated term. Path corrected on record: there is no single terms module, so this points at the governed gather body as the representative generated term.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard six, found by the third audit. A program name is not a lens name. Since coexistence landed one hyperschema carries several readings, so a door that gates on the program name authorizes every reading over that program including ones the operator never declared, while resolution proceeds by lens name. The tell for this class is a program name comparison sitting among lens name siblings.

Backfilled historical finding, hazard seven, authored and then violated within hours on the same day. An idempotence short circuit must prove it actually landed something rather than inferring completion from the presence of a marker, and an operation that discards the return value of the call that would tell it whether the work landed is reporting a success it did not achieve. This is the process layer failure that also makes feeding unearned passes to an evidence recorder unsafe.

The rewritten rail preconditions delta level leg was tautological. It asserted public declaration membership, which is a bare string set test over the literal the same file had just passed to the declare call, so no implementation of the door could make it fail. The fact the file needed was which registrations that string binds to on the public surface, which is where the lens versus program distinction actually lives. Third occurrence today of an honest looking comment claiming a stronger assertion than the code makes.

The rest rails header misstated the blast radius of the bug. Because a lens name falls back to the hyperschema name, a reading whose lens name coincides with its program name compares equal under both the correct and the reverted comparison, so the program name test silently keeps working for it and misfires only for a coexisting sibling. The fixture had named its broad reading identically to the program, making half the file insensitive in both directions while the header claimed both readings were affected.

The advertised structural defense for the operand set suppression hazard has zero callers. The hazards file names this helper as the safety net every new narrowing operation should run through, and no test file imports it, while the codebase ships at least four narrowing operations. A documented defense with no users is worse than none, because the next author reads the hazard, sees a named safety net, and nothing forces them through it. The helper also passes vacuously when the destination simply excludes the claim, so any caller must pair it with a crossing assertion.

A test named for the doors refusal of a non delta set term never reaches that check. One fixture term throws inside the evaluator before the door check is consulted and the other is asserted to be a valid delta set, so the doors own guard can be deleted entirely and the test stays green. The same guard is the shape check the quarantine pool relies on before seeding on a membership term, and the watch door has no rail at all.

A rail titled for a pinned renderer whose version is not declared public contains no such renderer in its fixture, so it re elaborates a different rails case instead. Its assertions exercise the latest branch rather than the pinned branch, meaning a pin check that wrongly admitted any undeclared pin would not be caught at the route door. The comment concedes the fixture cannot express the named scenario.

Genesis mints every lens under the program name while both other registration mint paths pass the lens name, and it silently discards the lens name field its own input type declares. Under coexistence a genesis declaring two readings over one hyperschema files both at the same living entity, so the latest per lens key collapses them and the last entry in the array wins with no error. Array order rather than the operator declared reading then decides which policy sits behind the anonymous door. Notably this was documented as an unavoidable constraint in a test file written hours earlier rather than recognized as a defect.

The withdrawn registration reader names each struck registration by the hyperschema program name while the door that consumes it compares against a lens name taken from the url, and every sibling comparison in that same function resolves through the lens. Two wrong outcomes follow: a withdrawn sibling reading answers a bare not found instead of the gone status the spec promises, degrading for every lens whose name differs from its program; and conversely a request naming the program can receive a gone that confirms a hash was lawful on the strength of a withdrawal belonging to a different reading.

The key that claims in its own comment to capture everything shaping the surface includes the program name and omits the lens name, though the lens determines the graphql type names, the rest path segment, and public door admission. It is latent only because the live mint paths derive the lens from the schema blob which is in the key; a binding whose lens name comes from the bytes rather than the schema breaks that coincidence, and the anonymous graphql cache is guarded solely by this key while the open set is invalidated separately.

The heal operation documents itself as finishing the forgetting on whatever tier the purge originally missed, but it only calls purge on a tier when that tier readable listing still hands back a dead delta. Readability and byte presence are exactly the two things the erasure section says must not be conflated. Because the earlier fix taught purge to sweep stragglers that the readable listing deliberately skips, once the canonical file is gone the straggler is invisible to the read, purge is never called, and that sweep is unreachable from the heal path on every subsequent start. Both purge calls also discard their counts so the report carries no signal about forgetting at all.

The two halves of the erasure fan out hold themselves to opposite standards and the primary path, the one that actually answers a data subject, is the lax one. The replica path states the doctrine outright and proves the tombstone landed before returning. The primary path performs the same three steps, discards the purge return value entirely, and returns a completeness claim about bytes derived from no evidence about bytes. A backend that removed zero rows produces a byte identical successful return.

Pull reconstructs each wire delta in a try catch and drops failures, then reports the offered count from the already filtered array, so a peer offering a hundred deltas of which forty will not reconstruct yields a report claiming sixty offered, sixty accepted, zero rejected. The drop itself is sanctioned by a deliberate divergence note but erasing it from the report is not. Because the failure is a property of the peer bytes rather than timing, the claim that the next pull heals is false: every later pull drops the same deltas and prints the same clean line.

The browser store purge removes only the canonical key while its own read path proves non canonical copies of a delta plaintext can exist and quarantines them, and the archive driver states the standard explicitly that purge must hunt every fan because a misfiled copy that stays readable means the delta was never forgotten. The removal count still increments for the canonical key so erasure reports success while legible bytes remain under a name only the quarantine listing mentions.

Row quarantine narrows the ground by setting aside unreadable rows, and it is the one narrowing that cannot carry a suppression closure because the dropped row is precisely what is illegible. When the dropped row is a negation its target boots live again, which revives retracted claims, restores write standing to revoked grants, and drops tombstones out of the forgotten set so an erased id passes the federation door. It is not self healing either: the unique id and ignore on conflict insert mean a healthy copy of the same negation cannot replace the corrupt one, and the repair listing never tells the operator that a strike was what fell out.

Translation copies a claim into a new delta citing its source, and suppression never follows that citation edge. The struck check runs only at emit time, so a retraction spoken after the pass leaves the canonical dialect copy live forever, served to local readers and federated outward. Re running the pass does not repair it because the pass only adds and the emission already exists. This is the operand set hazard at its most durable, since the narrowing is a re assertion rather than a filter and the retraction can never reach the copy at all.

The struck source gate applies constitutional standing to a data question, counting only operator authored negations, while every governed data reader honors the operator grantees as well. A granted tenant who retracts their own claim sees every reader treat it as struck, and the next translation pass still re renders it because that retraction was not operator authored, minting a fresh copy signed by the translator which the tenant has no standing to retract.

The pull request edit command failed silently twice and the tool reported nothing actionable, so a stale description stood for hours claiming four rails were sound when they had been found hollow and replaced. The underlying cause is that the command queries a deprecated projects surface and aborts, but the operational lesson is the same completeness hazard at the process layer: an operation reported success it did not achieve, and nobody verified the write. The fix used was to patch through the rest api and then read the result back to confirm. Any future automation that edits an external artifact must read it back.

The replica purge discards its count and its outcome, so a top level erase reports completion for bytes that may still be at rest inside a quarantine pool. The primary path now decides completeness from byte presence, and the replica path decides nothing at all, which is the same asymmetry the ticket set out to remove. A pool that never held the id and a pool that failed to remove it are indistinguishable to the caller.

The archive purge loops over ids crossed with fan directories doing an existence check per pair plus a prefix scan of each fan file list per pair. Heal now sweeps unconditionally with the whole accumulated tombstone set on every boot, so that cost grows monotonically with erasures and is never reclaimable since tombstones are append only. Inverting it to a single pass over the fan listings testing membership in a set of ids would bound it.

The H6 lint rule catches only DIRECT hyperschema.name comparisons, not a program name bound to a variable and then compared, which slips both the type checker and the rule. Not a behavior change and not the shape any H6 bug has taken; the comment was corrected to state the true scope rather than broaden the rule, since programOf and lensOf are already the ergonomic path and be-judicious-with-infrastructure argues against a heavier guard for a pattern that has never bitten.

The renderers.ts half of the pin fix was defense-in-depth the rail did not exercise: a deltaId is unique per delta so find-by-deltaId already returns the right version, and reverting the lens conjuncts left the test green. Closed by a rail that crafts a raw binding whose lens and version disagree and asserts the route door refuses rather than serving the sibling. Probed: reverting the conjunct turns it red.

The genesis collision key joined on a space while the reader it mirrors dedups on NUL, so a space-bearing entity or lens name could false-refuse a legitimate genesis. Fixed to join on NUL like the reader. Also removed a raw NUL byte that had ended up embedded in the source line (the T48 hazard - invisible to grep and diff), replacing it with a u0000 escape.

A named single-reading genesis whose schema name differs from the program name now mints a different living entity than before. Inert for the common case (anonymous schema or name equals program - proven by the green suite), but an existing store created pre-fix from such a genesis would gain a duplicate program-named lens on reboot because genesis is append-only re-minted. Additive not breaking, and the affected shape never worked (it is the H6 bug), so no deployed store relies on it - but it is a silent surface shift on upgrade and Myk should decide whether it wants a migration or documentation. STACKED for that reason.
